He has a recent    diagnosis of HIV ([**7-31**]), has C.diff with leukocytosis in 40s, adrenal    insufficiency, MDS(prbc and plt transfusion dependent), worsening    acidosis and renal failure due to ATN. results.summary:
Brief Hospital Course:
81yo male admitted MICU for septic shock (HIV, C. diff, adrenal insufficiency, MDS) with hypotension, lactate 5, and lactic acidosis.
Workup revealed DVT (left leg clots), AKI (Cr 2.7, BUN 81), and severe sepsis (temp 95-96F).
Treated with morphine, 2 units PRBC (Hct 28.8), IV abx, Bair Hugger, and renal consult; heparin held due to

results.source_note: Mr. [**Known lastname 8498**] is a 73 yoM on HD-anuric, h/o AFib not currently on    coumadin (though is on at home), who is being transferred from the    neurology service for respiratory distress and hypoxia.  He was    admitted on [**2191-8-12**] after a fall resulting in acute on chronic SDH on    the left; his course is complicated by seizure and he has been started    on fosphenytoin & phenytoin.    .    This evening he was noted to be in acute respiratory distress with    desats to the low to mid 90's on 3LNC (reportedly desatting to the mid    70's on room air; has had a variable O2 requirement since being    admitted).  His blood pressure dropped to the 70's systolic and was    responsive to the 80's and then 100's after a 250 cc NS bolus.  he was    febrile to 101.9,concern was aspiration event,pt has been on on    vanco/genta for the given recent fevers,with neg cultures     he was briefly in MICU green on [**8-17**] - [**8-18**] for fevers and hypotension    to the 70's.    Respiratory failure, acute (not ARDS/[**Doctor Last Name 2**])    Assessment:    Remains vented on ACx18/500/40%/5.  LS diminished at bases.  Sats    94-97% Pt sedated on fentanyl and versed drips.  He is able to follow    commands inconsistently.    Action:    Suctioned for thick copious secretions q 2-4 hours.    Response:    No change. Pt maintaining good saturation    Plan:    Wean vent as tolerated.  CXR in am.  ? a line placement if long term    ventilation needed.    Fever, unknown origin (FUO, Hyperthermia, Pyrexia)    Assessment:    TMAX  102.4 axillary.    Action:    Rectal Tylenol given.  Cooling blanket placed on pt.  Pt started on    cefepime.    Response:    Temperature decreased to 97.9.  Awaiting culture data.    Plan:    Administer Tylenol as needed.  Continue to monitor    Hypotension (not Shock)    Assessment:    HR 90 s SR.  BP 98-137/29-39.  Pt remains on levophed drip to maintain    systolic BPs >100.  Pt continues to have large residual from NGT so pt    remains NPO.  Elevated FS despite being NPO. Pt is HD dependent.  He    currently does not have access.    Action:    Attempted to wean levophed.  Able to decrease dose to .06mcgs/kg/min    from .12mcgs/kg/min.    Response:    Systolic goal >100.    Plan:    Wean levophed to off if tolerated.  Dialysis tomorrow. Pt will need    access placed.

73yoM transferred from neurology for resp distress/hypoxia post-fall SDH.
Admitted 2021-08-12 with acute on chronic SDH left, seizure, fever.
Treated with fosphenytoin/phenytoin, vanco/genta, vasopressors.
Brief MICU stay 08-17 to 08-18 for hypotension/fever.
Currently ventilated, febrile, hypotensive, anuric on HD.

Diagnoses:
Acute

results.source_note: 49 year old female with DM, HTN, CAD s/p 5-vessel CABG and PCI,    presented with L leg erythema/pain/swelling, fever, nausea/vomiting    since Wednesday.  In the ED she had a temp to 102.1 with rigors (was    given Motrin and tylenol) and was started on Vanc/Zosyn for presumptive    LLE cellulitis and admitted to medicine. Labs pertinent for CBC with    left shift, [**Last Name (un) **], hyponatremia and elevated lactate. Blood cultures grew    out STAPH AUREUS COAG + (MSSA) (4/4 bottles).  Hospital course    complicated by hypotension/Sepsis/ATN.  Pt transferred to MICU for    management of sepsis on.  Additionally, overnight patient developed RUE    "clumsiness" concerning for acute stroke, neurology was consulted and    MRI was negative for acute stroke.    Vascular surgery and podiatry were consulted for LLE management.    Vascular feel diabetic soft tissue infection and recommend IV    antibiotics. Podiatry feel acute charcot foot with overlying    cellulitis. They recommended betadine dressing to blister with dsd/ace    wrap daily, NWB and leg elevation    Sepsis without organ dysfunction    Assessment:    Tmax 99.5, WBC 10.2 Lactate this AM.  Off pressors since last evening.    [**3-29**] blood culture bottles + for MSSA    Action:    Currently on Zoysn, PICC line to be placed.  On ATC tylenol    Response:    No recent temp spikes    Plan:    Daily survelance cultures, ANBX as ordered    Renal failure, acute (Acute renal failure, ARF)    Assessment:    BUN 52 down from 54 Creat 4.0 (up from 3.7).  Phos up to 5.4  UOP 40-60    cc/hr clear yellow.   Renal US negative for hydronephrosis.    Action:    Currently on IVF at 100 cc/hr.  Lytes due at 1500.  Urine lytes sent    this AM, placed on renal diet    Response:    UOP increasing.    Plan:    Monitor urine output.  Bolus with IVF PRN for low UOP.  [**Hospital1 **] lytes    Impaired Skin Integrity/left lower extremity infection    Assessment:    Pt with open blister to LLE.  Ace    Action:    Being followed by vascular & podiatry.  DRSG [**Name5 (PTitle) 917**] this AM by    podiatry.  ANBX given    Response:    Plan:    NWB to LLE, Ace wrap to extremitie.  For Ct of extremity this AM.

results.summary:
Brief Hospital Course:
49F DM/HTN/CAD s/p CABG/PCI presented with LLE erythema, fever, rigors.
Admitted for sepsis (MSSA bacteremia) and hypotension.
Transferred to MICU; off pressors.
MRI negative for acute stroke.
Consults: vascular (diabetic soft tissue infection), podiatry (Charcot foot).

Diagnoses:
Sepsis, MSSA bacteremia, Acute kidney injury, Left lower extremity infection,
Charcot foot.

Procedures / interventions:

results.source_note: 87 yo male adm [**12-4**] with NSTEMI w/ new diastolic CHF in setting of    GIB. MI medically managed. IV lasix gtt for CHF, now resolved per CXR.    Sent to [**Hospital Ward Name 383**] 3 for a few days until [**12-11**] when temp spiked to 104 PR. Pt    found to have C-diff colitis. Abd CT with severe pancolitis versus    walled-off bowel perforation. Surgery following- no need for urgent    bowel surgery. NPO until further notice for bowel rest.  Pt still    awaiting PICC placement for TPN. IV nurse [**First Name (Titles) **] [**Last Name (Titles) 1892**] today and placed    PICC, however CXR shows line in coiled.  LINE LEFT IN PLACE- DO NOT    USE! To be advanced in IR tomorrow [**12-17**].  Has not eaten in at least 5    days. Spoke with team who ordered PPN until PICC placed for TPN.    Tachycardia/ Hypertension    Assessment:    S/P NSTEMI this admission. HR 70s-80s at rest, increased to 100 w/    activity. BP 130s-180s/70s.   These measurements in setting of    withholding PO cardiac meds for STRICT NPO status and IV lopressor 10mg    Q 4hours.    Action:    Team increased dose to 20mg IV lopressor Q4 hours.    Response:    Resting HR down to low 70s. BP still labile 130-160s/70s.    Plan:    Continue to monitor VS. Has only had one dose of lopressor 20mg IV.  If    HTN persists, check with about possibly adding another [**Doctor Last Name **].    C. difficile infection (C Diff, Cdiff Colitis, Clostridium Difficile)    Assessment:    4 liquid, yellow mucous-like stools today with streaking on sheets in    between. Perineal skin intact. Afebrile. WBC improved to 16 from 23.    Abdomen firm, distended. Hyperactive BSx4 quad.    Action:    Continue IV flagyl Q8 hours, PO vanco q 6 hours.    Response:    Decreasing # of stools. Afebrile.    Plan:    Continue to monitor.    Altered mental status (not Delirium)    Assessment:    Alert and oriented to person/ place/ time. Occasional forgetfulness.    Much improved MS now compared to when febrile a few days ago.    Action:    Bed alarms at all times, chair alarmed when OOB. Safety precautions.    Reoriented as needed.    Response:    Remained safe throughout shift. Improved confusion.    Plan:    Continue safety precautions.    Malnutrition    Assessment:    Pt without food x 5 days. First limited by new dysphasia in setting of    altered MS, now with new questionable bowel perforation so Strict NPO    and nothing per rectum  per surgery team until further notice.    Action:    Team alerted of concern re: malnourishment.  PICC line ordered for TPN-    IV nurse [**First Name (Titles) **] [**Last Name (Titles) 1892**] and placed PICC, however coiled per Xray.  Line    left in L AC until IR can repair tomorrow.  Team alerted- ordered PPN.    Response:    PPN initiated at 1700.    Plan:    PPN for now. To IR tomorrow for PICC, then start TPN.

results.summary:
Brief Hospital Course:
87M admitted 12-4 with NSTEMI, new diastolic CHF, GIB.
CHF resolved per CXR; MI medically managed.
Temp spiked 104 PR 12-11; C. diff colitis diagnosed.
Abd CT showed severe pancolitis vs walled-off perforation.
No urgent bowel surgery needed; NPO for bowel rest.
PICC placed 12-17 but coiled on CXR; line left in place.
Awaiting IR advancement tomorrow for PICC repair.
Has not eaten

results.source_note: Ms. [**Known lastname 861**] is a 81 year old woman admitted to T/SICU from the OR    following a subtotal colectomy with an end ileostomy for bowel    ischemia.    *admitted to [**Hospital1 19**] [**8-5**] for NSTEMI Mi, cath d and angioplasty done of    RCA (had R groin hematoma post procedure)    *on [**8-12**] had rapid afib (rate to 150 s), converted to sinus with    digoxin and then 30 minutes after confusion began complaining of    abdominal pain.  CT of abdomen done and was concerning for ischemic    bowel.  To OR for resection.    Postop:    Initially stable then developed rapid afib to 180 with hypotension    [**8-13**], failed electrical cardioversion, cardioverted with amio.    In and out of afib. [**Last Name (un) 3864**] placed, bedside echo done.    Atrial fibrillation (Afib)    Assessment:    At start of shift was in SR with HR 60 s. SBP 110-120 s. MAP >60 Amio    drip infusing @ 0.5mg/min.    Action:    Amio gtt continues. Started milrinone bolus and patient became more    hypotensive, so that was held. Diltiazem drip started with attempt to    rate control and titrated between 5-15 mg/hr. metoprolol 5 mg IV given    x1.    Response:    Afib with RVR best rate controlled after 5mg IV lopressor, and    diltiazem now titrated down to 5mg/hr. patient converted to SR    mid-morning and now with HR in the 60s. Levo weaned off and neo    titrated up to maintain MAP 60    Plan:    PTT pending for heparin titration.  continue NEO  to support BP as    needed and continue dilt drip at 5 mg/hr, with metoprolol PRN for    increased HR.    Pain control (acute pain, chronic pain)    Assessment:    Fentanyl drip titrated to maintain pain control.    Action:    Titrated down for vetn wean. Fentanyl boluses of 25mcg before turns.    Response:    Continues to grimace with turns but rests quietly when undisturbed.    Tolerating wean well.    Plan:    Continue fentanyl for pain with increased dose for turning and    procedures.    Myocardial infarction, acute (AMI, STEMI, NSTEMI)    Assessment:    Afib as above.  Cool extremities. CO adequate [**First Name8 (NamePattern2) **] [**Last Name (un) 3864**]. SV down to    40cc while she is in afib. Increase in cr from 0.6 yesterday to 1.8    today. Adequate urine output. Weight up to 60kg (preop 50kg).  Hands    and feet edematous. Remains vented.    Action:    Mouth care per vap protocol. Q 1hour hemodynamics. Levophed changed to    neo to support bp with less beta agonist effect. Family updated with    plan and condition. TTE preformed today.  Diltiazem drip on for HR    control.    Response:    Continues to be pressor dependent. Continues to have activity    intolerance, improved since last night. Generalized edema noted and cr    increased indicating poor perfusion though urine output adequate.    Awaiting formal read of TTE     Plan:    Continue to maximize CO/CI with use of pressors/IVF boluses PRN.  Wean    pressors if indicated and continue to closely [**Initials (NamePattern4) **] [**Last Name (NamePattern4) **] [**Last Name (un) 3864**] numbers.    Intestinal ischemia (including mesenteric venous / arterial thrombosis,    bowel ischemia)    Assessment:    Midline incision with R sided end ileostomy. Remains npo. Ileostomy    pink with small amount of golden stool..  Afebrile, wbc flat.    Continues to have tenderness and guarding in belly on exam.  Ng with    minimal output    Action:    Antibiotics dc d today. Abdominal exam remains unremarkable. Stoma    nurse consulted and will see patient tomorrow.    Response:    Probable incisional pain in abdomen.  Hypoactive bowel sounds. Small    amount of ileostomy output. Stoma appears healthy    Plan:    Cont to follow fever curve and return of bowel function.  ??nutrition    whether she will soon tolerate enteral feedings or need tpn. Stoma    nurse to see patient tomorrow.

results.summary:
Brief Hospital Course:
81yo F admitted for NSTEMI, RCA PCI with groin hematoma.
Developed rapid AFib, converted to SR with digoxin.
Admitted for bowel ischemia, subtotal colectomy, end ileostomy.
Post-op developed rapid AFib, hypotension, failed cardioversion.
Cardioverted with amiodarone, converted to SR.
Pressors weaned from levophed to norepinephrine.
Discharged to home or rehab pending disposition.

Diagnoses:
Acute NSTEMI, RCA angioplasty, groin hematoma.
Atrial fibrillation with rapid ventricular response.
Bowel ischemia, subtotal colectomy, end ileostomy.
Acute kidney injury

results.source_note: Chief Complaint:  hypotension    HPI:    70yo f w SCLC, DMII/HTN/Hyperlipidemia, afib on coumadin, ESRD 2'    previous cisplatin therapy, transferred to unit for hypotension.    .    Hosp course initially p/w SOB and found to have large right pleural    effusion.  Tapped (transudative on [**6-25**]).  On [**7-13**], had right mainstem    bronch stent placed.  This was c/b hypoxic resp failure and need for    intubation.  She was in the ICU and underwent [**Initials (NamePattern4) **] [**Last Name (NamePattern4) **] on [**7-14**] (900cc    removed).  She tolerated this well and was sent to the East floors.    This week c/b rising WBC and concerns for postob PNA.  Recently started    on vanco and levaquin to treat for PNA and patient remained stable on    floor.  Over last 3d has had constipation and increasing abd pain.  Rx    at one point with manual disimpaction w success.    .    Today, she had increased abd pain.  lactate was 2.6.  CT abd showed    ileus but no acute mesenteric stranding or ischemia.  CT abd showed RLL    consolidation with effusion.  Tonight at 7pm, had acute worsening abd    pain and found to have SBP 60s (down from baseline 90-100).  Pt had    decreased ms and poor access so HD line was used for 1.3 L bolus.  SBP    improved to 110    In MICU, art line confirmed SBP 90-110.  Abd pain persisted and was    largely relieved by manual disimpaction.    Hypotension (not Shock)    Assessment:    Manual bp variable, 70-90s.    Action:    2L NS x2 given during transfer, aline placed.  Additional 500cc NS    bolus given    Response:    Abp 90s-110s    Plan:    Cont to monitor bp. Bolus PRN. Aline positional.    Constipation (Obstipation, FOS)    Assessment:    Abdom firm, c/o [**10-27**] pain.  CT showed ileus.    Action:    Lactulose given, manual disimpaction x2, fleets enema given. NGT    attempted, mushroom cath placed.    Response:    Small amount of stool removed from disimpacting. No response from    lactulose and enema. Pt vomited when NGT placement attempt. Moderate    relief emesis and disimpation.    Plan:    ?NGT placement, disimpacted PRN.    Neuro: alert and oriented. c/o [**10-27**] abdominal pain. Pain meds held r/t    unstable bp. Afebrile (bld cx sent).    Resp: NC 2L, LSC diminished at bases.    CV: sbp 90s-110s, total of 2.5LNS bloused. NSR-ST, converted to afib    150s-160s during NGT attempt. 5mg metoprololx2 given.    GI/GU: anuric, HD Tues/Thurs/Sat. constipated, mushroom cath placed.

results.summary:
Brief Hospital Course:
70yo f w SCLC, DM2, HTN, ESRD transferred for hypotension.
Initial workup showed large right pleural effusion (transudative).
Right mainstem bronch stent placed for hypoxic resp failure.
Developed constipation, rising WBC, and suspected postob PNA.
Treated with vanco/levaquin; remained stable on floor.
Acute worsening abd pain, SBP 60s, lactate 2.6.
CT abd showed ileus, RLL consolidation with effusion.

results.source_note: Pt is a 74 yo admitted to MICU 6 with PMH of HTN, Hypothyroidism,    gallstones, and hyperlipidemia who presented to ED from home with c/o    increasing LE edema, increasing SOB over last few days.  Pt recently    dx. with ovarian CA vs. Lymphoma and is undergoing workup work-up to    date shows ovarian adenocarcinoma, all data is not in yet.    Pre-admission abdominal CT with dye load and recent labs revealed    worsening kidney failure so PCP [**Name (NI) 167**] pt at home and sent pt    directly to ED.  On admission BUN= 116 and creatinine = 7.2 Pt SOB and    placed on 4L NC 02 with Sats= 94-96%, K= 7.8 and received kaexylate    with insulin and D50 and calcium gluconate.  CXR revealed new Large    Left pleural effusion ->>thorocentesis for 1L bloody fluid, and chest    tube placed. Worsening respiratory status d/t fluid overload from ARF,    pleural effusions, ascites,  peri-cardial effusion. Pt started on CRRT    [**9-16**]. BIPAP on/off. 2^nd left pigtail chest tube placed [**9-17**] with 960    cc bloody drainage out.    Shock, hypovolemic or hemorrhagic    Assessment:    SBP 90-100 with MAP > 65. PT a+o x 3. HR 94-110 ST.    Action:    Weaned levophed down from .08mcgs/kg/min to .06 mcgs/kg/min    Response:    SBP falling to low 70s and MAP 55-58    Plan:    Continue levophed to maintain MAP > 60.    Renal failure, acute (Acute renal failure, ARF)    Assessment:    Tolerating CRRT for fluid removal rate of 50ccs/hr. TFB neg 1250ccs    yest. BUN/Cr down to 31/1.9 respectively. Urine output improving    5-35ccs/hr via foley.    Action:    Attempted to increase goal removal rate to 100ccs/hr.    Response:    BP fell with MAP < 60 and filter and effluent pressures increased.    Plan:    Remain with goal fluid removal rate of 50ccs/hr. Follow labs and    replete lytes per CRRT protocol.    Pleural effusion, acute    Assessment:    02 sats 99-100% on 4 L NC. LS clear, diminished at bases. ABG    7.41/44/153/3/29. Chest tube #1 with no drainage. Sm amt sanguionous    drainage from chest tube #2(pig-tail).    Action:    O2 decrease to 2 L NC.    Response:    Sats remain 98-100%. RR 18-32. Pt denies sob.    Plan:    Surgery to remove chest tube #1 today and ? perform pleuradesis with CT    #2.    Pain control (acute pain, chronic pain)    Assessment:    Pt denies pain at c-tube insertion sites. c/o back discomfort from    lying in bed.    Action:    Repositioned freq. Tylenol given. Initially refused ativan but    eventually agreed to try it. Declined oxycodone.    Response:    Pt c/o slight improvement in back discomfort but still awake most of    the night.    Plan:    Encourage use of Tylenol and ativan at bedtime. Freq repositioning and    skin care.

results.summary:
Brief Hospital Course:
74yo F admitted MICU with ovarian CA, AKI, hypovolemic shock, bilateral pleural effusions.
Presented with LE edema, SOB; pre-admission CT showed worsening renal failure.
Admitted with BUN 116, Cr 7.2, K 7.8, hypoxia; received kayexalate, insulin, D50, calcium.
Thoracentesis yielded 1L bloody fluid; chest tube placed.
Started CRRT for fluid overload; weaned levophed due to hypotension.
2nd

results.source_note: This is an 80 yo female w/PMed Hx of CAD, CABG, DMwho came from the heb    Reb with SOB.  At Heb Reb she had 1 week of fevers and altered mental    status, her alter MS coincided with the administration of baclofen and    dilaudid  these were D/Ced.  On [**3-8**] her 02 SAT was 80% on RA and 90%    on 3 liters; this initiated her transfer to [**Hospital1 54**].  In the EW her temp    was 100.8, 163/73,101, 18 99% on 3 liters.  An EKG showed 1mm ST    depressions in V4-5, BNP was 4005 and her CXR showed multifocal    pneumonia.     Pt ruled out for MI this admission. TTE showed diastolic dysfunction.    Continues to struggle with hypoxia/resp distress with absolutely no    reserve..  Supplemental O2 is maxed out at 95% high flow neb with    frequent episodes tachypnea to 40 s. Pt restless at times. Team would    like to get chest CT and bronchial lavage but feel she would need to be    intubated for either of these procedures. They plan to insert A-line    today.    Pt Complains of chronic back and neck pain. Receives PRN Percocet and    TID neurontin 300mg for the complaints of pain.    Access has been an issue today. Her midline was phlebitic and needed to    be d/c d. Warm compresses applied Q4hr. I was not able to insert new    PIV> IVT came and got second PIV placed and later came to insert PICC    in left AC.    Pneumonia, bacterial, hospital acquired (non-VAP)    Assessment:    Action:    Response:    Plan:    Heart failure (CHF), Diastolic, Acute    Assessment:    Pt s vital signs stable on lisinopril 40mg PO QD as well as lopressor    25mg PO BID for rate/BP control    Action:    Checking BP on lower right arm due to phlebitis on upper right and new    PICC in left AC    Response:    BP is stable. Will hold off on diuresis for now because pt is    co,mplaining of thirst and creatinine is slightly higher today.    Plan:    Consider diuresis later if needed. Continue to follow labs/vital signs    closely.    Pain control (acute pain, chronic pain)    Assessment:    Pt complains of left neck, shoulder, back pain constantly.    Action:    Gets slight relief from one percocet and one Tylenol given twice this    shift. Also pt repositioned and given back rub with increased comfort.    Response:    Slight improvement in comfort from percocet, Tylenol and position    change with back rub.    Plan:    Continue pain relief measures as tolerated.    Numerous family members in to see pt today (she has six children) I    have asked them to  limit visit time and numbers because pt will not    stop wanting to interact with them and becomes hypoxic easily while    they visit. She also has coughing jags while she tries to talk and    desaturates easily. Family seems to understand and comply with my    wishes but with so many here it is difficult for her to rest. We will    need to continue to request that they limit pt stimulation.
results.source_preview: This is an 80 yo female w/PMed Hx of CAD, CABG, DMwho came from the heb Reb with SOB. At Heb Reb she had 1 week of fevers and altered mental status, her alter MS coincided with the administration of baclofen and dilaudid these were D/Ced. On [**3-8**] her 02 SAT was 80% on RA and 90% on 3 liters; this initiated her transfer to [**Hospital1 54**]. In the EW her temp was 100.8, 163/73,101, 18 99% on 3 liters. An EKG showed 1mm ST depressions in V4-5, BNP was 4005 and her CXR showed multifocal p...
results.summary:
Brief Hospital Course:
80yo F admitted for SOB, fever, AMS, Hx pneumonia.
Temp 100.8, HTN 163/73, HR 101, SpO2 99% on 3L O2.
EKG 1mm ST depressions V4-5; BNP 4005; CXR multifocal pneumonia.
TTE diastolic dysfunction; MI ruled out.
Maxed O2 95% HFNC; tachypnea 40; restless.
Plan A-line, CT, BAL; likely intubation.
PICC

results.source_note: This is a 71 y.o male with 6 week hx. Of diarrhea (non bloody) and    weakness. Pt. underwent screening colonoscopy on [**11-1**] which revealed    an obstructing/bleeding hepatic flexure mass. Bx done at that time.    PMhx.    CHF, HTN, Hyperlipedemia, COPD, DM II, ischemic heart dz., s/p CABG x2    in [**2158**].    Today Pt. went to OR for scheduled lap colectomy that turned into open    colectomy. Pt. failed extubation in OR and reintubated there. Also Pt.    had been hypotensive to 60 s systolic and non responsive to neo    boluses. Started on Neo gtt. CXR consistent with CHF and pt. recived 40    mg IV lasix in PACU.  EBL minimal per PACU report.    .H/O heart failure (CHF), Diastolic, Acute on Chronic    Assessment:    Pt. failed extubation in OR. CXR showed CHF. Recived Lasix 40 mg in    PACU. Hypotensive and on Neo upon transfer from PACU. Propofol weaned    down from 25mcg to 15mcg for sedation.    Action:    Repeat cxr in MICU. Neo weaned off and BP stable at 100 s systolic.    Response:    Maintaining BP at 110 s/50 s with MAP>70. HR 70 s NSR with no ectopy    noted. Second set of cardiac enzymes sent at 1800 and pending.    Plan:    Respiratory failure, acute (not ARDS/[**Doctor Last Name 2**])    Assessment:    Hx. Of COPD. Failed extubation post surgery.  Recived from PACU    intubated on A/C 460x 14 PEEP5 and 60% FiO2. LS clear and diminished at    bases.  ABG 7.30/54/193 at 1700.    Action:    Vent changed to CPAP 5/5 with 40%.    Response:    Repeat ABG at 1800    Plan:    [**Last Name 19**] Problem  s/p open colectomy    Assessment:    Pt. s/p open colectomy today for obstructive mass.  Low abd. Midline    incision covered with DSD and tegaderm. Srtyker pump infusing    Bupivicane to wound.    Action:    Monitor incision site for s/s of bleeding.    Response:    Dsg. Remains CDI. BS hypoactive. Pt. NPO.  Pt. shakes head no when    asked if in pain.    Plan:    Cont. to monitor. Maintain pump for 72hr as ordered. Surgery will    remove pump.
results.source_preview: This is a 71 y.o male with 6 week hx. Of diarrhea (non bloody) and weakness. Pt. underwent screening colonoscopy on [**11-1**] which revealed an obstructing/bleeding hepatic flexure mass. Bx done at that time. PMhx. CHF, HTN, Hyperlipedemia, COPD, DM II, ischemic heart dz., s/p CABG x2 in [**2158**]. Today Pt. went to OR for scheduled lap colectomy that turned into open colectomy. Pt. failed extubation in OR and reintubated there. Also Pt. had been hypotensive to 60 s systolic and non respons...
results.summary:
Brief Hospital Course:
71M with 6-wk diarrhea/weakness admitted for obstructing hepatic flexure mass.
Failed extubation post open colectomy; hypotensive to 60s SBP refractory to norepi.
Received 40 mg IV lasix for CHF; intubated on A/C 460x14 PEEP5 60% FiO2.
Vent switched to CPAP 5/5 40%; BP stabilized >100s SBP.

Diagnoses:
Obstructive hepatic flexure mass

results.source_note: Patient w/ recent hx of ERCP; Had an elective lap chole on [**1-22**] at    [**Hospital **] [**Hospital **] Hospital and post op was admitted to the floor; on    POD #1 pt had increasing abdominal pain, became hypotensive with    decreased uop. Patient taken back to the OR for an ex lap- found a    cystic stump leak from clips falling off postop; her small bowel was    also dusky and she underwent a SBR;; patient was transferred to TSICU    for further management of care on [**1-24**]. aggressive resuscitation,    triple pressures then returned to OR that night where more ischemic    bowel was found and a transverse colectomy was preformed.  Pt taken    back to OR [**1-25**] for resection of terminal ileum, ascending, transverse    colon and upper portion of the sigmoid colon. [**1-26**] pt back to OR for a    resection of bowel anastamosis, reanastamosis, ileostomy, bilat    oophorectomy, salpingectomy, partial hysterectomy, [**State **] patch,    g-tube, and J-tube. [**1-27**] started on CVVH. CVVH was stopped [**1-29**] due to    persistent hypotension    Sepsis, Severe (with organ dysfunction)    Assessment:    +blood cultures for yeast    Abdomen firm, open, absent BS,  abdominal [**Doctor Last Name **] drains put out large    amnt secretions,    SBP >90    Black tongue    Large amnt of oozing from every skin breakdown    Low platelets    Pt withdraws to pain, grimaced to pain/suctioning, does not open eyes,    poor gag/cough,    Large NGT output, absent BS, abd firm/open. Less output from G-tube    Action:    Off Levophed last night at [**2122**]    Got 1 unit of platlets  transfused overnight    [**Last Name (un) 3864**] monitoring is ongoing(team aware that Pt is in a/fib and this    might impact [**Last Name (un) 13380**] accuracy)    Pt was blood cultured last night    Remains on meropenum, vanco, cipro and micafungin    Treated with fentanyl for pain    Response:    SVV 18-24, SBP >90, normothermic, warm feet, + pulses, tongue remains    black, PLT count up to >80    Plan:    Cont antibiotics, hemodynamics monitoring,    Respiratory failure, acute (not ARDS/[**Doctor Last Name 11**])    Assessment:    Intubated day 5. Remains on a/c with 10 peep, LS with    rhonchi/diminished    Action:    VAP care, monitoring ABGs, self rotating bed, unable to obtain RSBI due    to PEEP    Response:    No changed in vent settings, ABG wnl    Plan:    Full vent support    [**Last Name **] Problem  alteration skin integrity    Assessment:    Pt has multiple puncture wounds that leaking as well as many    blisters-some of which have bursted.    Mepeflex dsg on coccyx intact,    open abdomen with clear dsg on and JP/[**Doctor Last Name **] drains intact,    Action:    Skin care, rotating bed, central lipid TPN is ongoing    Response:    Less drainage from R. groin bag, c.line dsdg was changed twice    Plan:    keep pt s skin dry, frequent skin care    Renal failure, acute (Acute renal failure, ARF)    Assessment:    Creat >2.1-up from 1.9 yeasterday, lactate 5 today-up from 4.7    Action:    CVVHDF off yesterday.    Response:    Worsening renal picture while off CVVHDf    Plan:    Consult with renal team, monitor I/O, replete lytes as needed    Atrial fibrillation (Afib)    Assessment:    Pt remains in A/fib, 85-100 rate, irregular.    Action:    Amioderone gtt ongoing at 0.5mg /min    Response:    No changes in rate    Plan:    Consider cardioversion
results.source_preview: Patient w/ recent hx of ERCP; Had an elective lap chole on [**1-22**] at [**Hospital **] [**Hospital **] Hospital and post op was admitted to the floor; on POD #1 pt had increasing abdominal pain, became hypotensive with decreased uop. Patient taken back to the OR for an ex lap- found a cystic stump leak from clips falling off postop; her small bowel was also dusky and she underwent a SBR;; patient was transferred to TSICU for further management of care on [**1-24**]. aggressive resuscitation...
results.summary:
Brief Hospital Course:
- Post-op day 1 developed abdominal pain, hypotension, decreased urine output.
- Exploratory lap found cystic stump leak, dusky small bowel; underwent SBR.
- Transferred to TSICU; returned OR for ischemic bowel resection and colectomy.
- Day 1-26 underwent multiple bowel resections, anastomosis, ileostomy, hysterectomy.
- Day 27 started CVVH for worsening renal failure; stopped day 29 due to hypotension.
- Intubated day 5; remains on full ventilator support with PEEP 10.
- Persistent seps
